Very likely the same underlying function as the bare global `StringToGuid` used pervasively elsewhere in the corpus, though the aliasing evidence is one-directional — strong for StringToGuid, unconfirmed for GuidToString.
natives.Sys.SubtitlesEnabled: Returns whether subtitles are enabled; used both guarded and unguarded, read in the pause-screen options menu.
natives.Sys.TimeStampGetElapsed: Returns elapsed seconds since a timestamp handle (from MainTimeStamp/RealTimeStamp, re-markable via TimeStampMark) was last marked. Forms a poll-based elapsed-time primitive, an alternative to the callback-based Event.TimerRelative for code that needs to check elapsed time on demand.
natives.Sys.TimeStampMark: Resets/re-marks an existing timestamp handle in place. Call sites consistently re-mark a stamp previously produced by MainTimeStamp/RealTimeStamp rather than creating a new one.
natives.Sys.TutorialsEnabled: Returns whether the tutorial-hint HUD message system is enabled. This is the gate checked in MrxTutorialManager before showing or updating a tutorial popup message, and it's read defensively in the pause-screen options-menu toggle.
natives.Sys.YAxisInverted: Returns whether the Y-axis is inverted; read alongside RumbleEnabled/SubtitlesEnabled in the same pause-screen options read.
natives.Vehicle.CancelHijack: Takes a plain character guid; cancels an active hijack. Observed being called on player logout/cleanup.
natives.Vehicle.ClearControls: Takes a plain vehicle guid; clears the vehicle's controls, called alongside turret-disable logic during hijack setup.
natives.Vehicle.CloseDoor: Takes a vehicle guid and a door/part name string (e.g. "pivot"); mirrors OpenDoor to close it.
natives.Vehicle.EnableTurret: Takes a vehicle guid, a turret/part name ("head", "main_turret"), a boolean, and sometimes an axis string ("pitch", "all") plus another boolean. Confirmed to be control/orientation-only, not weapon selection: every real call site just disables or re-enables the turret's own control during a hijack cinematic and never touches what the turret fires.
natives.Vehicle.Enter: Takes a vehicle guid, character guid, a seat-type string ("d", "p"), and one or two boolean flags (immediate, plus an optional extra flag). Puts a character into a vehicle by seat type rather than a specific seat guid.
natives.Vehicle.EnterBySeatGuid: Takes a vehicle guid, character guid, seat guid, and one or two boolean flags (immediate, plus an extra flag); returns a success boolean. Puts a character into a specific seat identified by guid.
natives.Vehicle.Exit: Takes a vehicle guid and a character guid, frequently with a trailing boolean flag; removes the character from the vehicle.
natives.Vehicle.GetDriver: Returns the driver's character guid for a vehicle uGuid, or nil if unmanned. Very widely used in real game scripts, often guarded with a nil check.
natives.Vehicle.GetFromRider: Takes a character uGuid; returns the vehicle guid the character is currently riding in, or nil if the character is on foot.
natives.Vehicle.GetRiders: Takes a vehicle uGuid and an optional seat-type filter string (e.g. "p", "g", "driver"); returns a table of rider guids.
natives.Vehicle.GetSeatByType: Takes a vehicle uGuid and a seat-type string ("p", "d"); at least one real call site passes a third boolean argument as well.
natives.Vehicle.GetSeatFromRider: Takes a character uGuid; returns the guid of the seat that character occupies.
natives.Vehicle.GetSeatParams: Takes a seat guid; returns a params table that includes at least an IsGunner field per observed usage.
natives.Vehicle.GetSeatToSeat: Takes a seat guid and a boolean; appears to return possible seat-transfer targets from that seat.
natives.Vehicle.HijackAbort: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; aborts an in-progress hijack.
natives.Vehicle.HijackAbortDone: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; signals that a hijack-abort has finished.
natives.Vehicle.HijackComplete: Takes a plain character (hijacker) guid. Part of the hijack state machine; signals a successful hijack completion.
natives.Vehicle.HijackStart: Takes a hijacker guid, hijackee guid, vehicle guid, and (in the one observed call site) a fourth argument that was a table/object reference (self). Begins the hijack state machine.
natives.Vehicle.IsFlying: Takes a plain vehicle guid; returns a boolean, checked before flight-specific hijack logic runs.
natives.Vehicle.IsHijackRemote: Takes a character guid; checks whether a hijack is remote. Its one real call site guards the call itself with `Vehicle.IsHijackRemote and ...`, implying the function's presence was historically uncertain in some contexts.
natives.Vehicle.IsSeatALadder: Takes a plain seat guid; returns a boolean for whether that seat is a ladder-type seat.
natives.Vehicle.IsSeatBlocked: Takes a plain seat guid; returns a boolean for whether the seat is currently blocked/unusable.
natives.Vehicle.OpenDoor: Takes a vehicle guid and a door/part name string (e.g. "pivot", "DriverHatch"). Using it to open a tank hatch ("DriverHatch") has been proposed as a live test but is not yet confirmed working.
natives.Vehicle.SetCanPlayerUse: Takes a vehicle guid, a seat-type string ("d", "a"), and a boolean; controls whether a player can use that seat type.
natives.Vehicle.SetHijackState: Takes a character guid and a numeric state value; sets the hijacker's state within the hijack state machine (exact state numbering not documented from call sites).
natives.Vehicle.SetHijackSuccess: Takes a character guid and a boolean (observed as false in the corpus); records hijack success/failure for that character.
natives.Vehicle.SetParts: Takes a vehicle/object guid, a part name string (e.g. "LightFront", "CtrlRotation"), and a boolean; returns a boolean. Also seen used on non-vehicle gate/alarm objects, suggesting broader applicability than just player vehicles.
natives.Vehicle.SetTurretPitch: Takes a vehicle guid, turret name, and a numeric pitch value (observed as 0, resetting pitch to level right after disabling the turret during hijack). Orientation only, same caveat as EnableTurret — does not affect firing.
natives.Vehicle.StopTankHijackMotion: Takes a plain vehicle guid; stops the tank hijack motion/animation sequence.
natives.Vehicle.TransferToSeat: Takes a vehicle guid, a seat guid, and a boolean flag; transfers a rider between seats.
natives.Vehicle.Usable: Takes a vehicle guid and a boolean; toggles whether the vehicle can be entered/used at all.
natives.Weapon.GetMaxReserveAmmo: Returns the maximum reserve ammo capacity for a carried weapon guid (uWeapon). Almost always paired with SetReserveAmmo in the idiom Weapon.SetReserveAmmo(uWeapon, Weapon.GetMaxReserveAmmo(uWeapon)) to refill a weapon to full.
natives.Weapon.GetReserveAmmo: Returns the current reserve ammo count for a carried/equipped weapon guid (uWeapon). Always called with a player's hand-held weapon, never a vehicle weapon.
natives.Weapon.SetReserveAmmo: Sets the reserve ammo of a carried weapon guid (uWeapon) to nAmount. Commonly used to refill a weapon to max via Weapon.SetReserveAmmo(uWeapon, Weapon.GetMaxReserveAmmo(uWeapon)), but is also seen setting a flat value like 1; it can also be registered directly as an Event.Create callback (e.g. for ObjectHibernation), so it may be invoked by the engine's event dispatcher rather than called directly.
